items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed near Speedy's Tacos location
items.summary: Sunnyvale is considering a 67-unit residential project (townhomes and apartments) combined with 18,535 square feet of retail on a 4.22-acre site. The development would keep the existing Speedy's Tacos building and add a new two-story retail building, representing significant infill growth in the area.

items.headline: Protected tree removal permit challenged at 751 Waverley Street
items.summary: A previously approved permit to remove protected trees at 751 Waverley Street is undergoing director review, suggesting community concerns about the tree removal decision.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates for new fiscal year
items.summary: The City Council considered revisions to business license tax rates in the municipal code, with the finance department presenting the annual update. This affects local businesses operating in Campbell and may influence business costs in the community.

items.headline: Council addresses affordability protections when housing subsidies expire
items.summary: The council deferred discussion on how the city will protect affordability in housing when project-based subsidy contracts end or properties face foreclosure. This matters to residents concerned about preservation of affordable housing stock in San José.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara plans temporary supergraphic signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council considered an ordinance to allow large supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium during the FIFA World Cup 2026 events. The item was withdrawn by staff, but signals the city is preparing for World Cup activities and temporary visual modifications to the stadium area.

items.headline: City Council approves FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
items.summary: Milpitas updated its development impact fees and utility rates for the upcoming fiscal year. These changes affect what new construction projects and residents pay for city services.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness plan to Milpitas council
items.summary: County officials presented their Community Plan to End Homelessness to the city council on April 21. This addresses how the region will tackle homelessness, which affects South Bay communities.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1: Five new homes approved on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: San José approved five new single-family homes in the Comm Hill development's Phase 3.1, ranging from $487k to $573k, all issued the same day on Altino Boulevard. This represents a significant addition to housing supply in an active residential development project.
